hard.next_step: Send BT a tailored information pack (product collateral, demo videos, solution overviews on AI sourcing, procurement orchestration and savings management); after Shane reviews internally, follow up on feedback and explore a future RFP or a live demo.

north_star_verdict.headline: Six-day-old Qualified S2C deal that is alive but on the outside of BT Sourced's active evaluation. Shane Gallagher confirmed on the 30 June intro that both the orchestration and AI-sourcing RFI windows had already closed (submissions in, over a month old), so Zycus is chasing a seat in a future RFP it does not yet hold. The only forward motion is a rep-side 'Next Steps' follow-up emailed to Shane on 1 July, still awaiting his reply; no demo or next meeting is booked, no economic buyer is engaged, and Zycus is single-threaded on a gatekeeper. Not cold (the deal is days old), but momentum from the single intro call has not converted.
north_star_verdict.math: Close date 30 June 2027 (~359 days out, a year-long placeholder). Created and qualified 30 June 2026; 0 days progressed beyond Qualified. One buyer touch: the Zycus <> BTS intro call 30 June 2026, the same day the opp was created. One rep-side follow-up email 1 July 2026 (LastActivityDate), awaiting buyer reply. No forward slip (close date unchanged since creation). Pace: a full year to close, but BT's orchestration and AI-sourcing RFIs already closed ~late May 2026 without Zycus, so the near-term risk is exclusion from the RFP, not the date.
evidence: Shane Gallagher (BT Sourced, Solutions, buyer) said on 30 June 2026 the orchestration RFI had closed with submissions in and evaluation underway, and the AI-sourcing RFI had also closed: 'the window has been missed for the RFI for the orchestration, which went out, you know, about a month ago, over a month ago' (Avoma Zycus <> BTS Intro, 30 June 2026).

Shane asked for materials to review internally before any demo: 'if you could send over some materials even so that I can review and kind of bring it to a couple of people before demo' (Avoma, 30 June 2026).
Anthony Gray (Zycus) noted BT would likely invite peers to the RFP: 'I know you'll probably be inviting people like Zip and GEP... we're winning in that space' (Avoma, 30 June 2026).
A 'Next Steps' follow-up email was sent to Shane on 1 July 2026 (Clari, LastActivityDate 2026-07-01), thanking him for connecting with Anthony and Claire; no buyer reply logged yet.
Only two Influencer contact roles exist (Shane Gallagher, primary, and Simen Wefald); no Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or Champion role is populated and no budget owner has joined a call.

metrics.narrative: Shane Gallagher articulated a qualitative savings-tracking pain but no quantified target: 'what is of interest to us is like the sort of development of an initiative through its life cycle and kind of capturing the delta throughout the different stage gates to kind of understand and maybe have a bit more context' (Avoma, 30 June 2026). He framed the current state as an information wash: 'historically, that context would be a wash because no one can sit down and go through all that. But actually now with sort of the brute force of AI, we can kind of find insights in that' (Avoma, 30 June 2026). No savings target, cost-reduction goal, cycle-time, headcount, or ROI figure was named, and no metric was attached to the orchestration or AI-sourcing pain. The move is to quantify the gap (number of savings initiatives, manual review hours, lost savings visibility, request/intake volume) to build a defensible business case.
sources: Avoma Zycus <> BTS Intro, 30 June 2026 (Shane Gallagher); carried from prior canonical record

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is identified or engaged. Salesforce lists only two Influencer contact roles (Shane Gallagher, primary, and Simen Wefald) and no Economic Buyer, Executive Sponsor, or budget-owner role. The prior call surfaced that the former CPO, Cyril, departed around January 2026 and a CPO-equivalent now owns the procurement-transformation budget, but that person is unnamed in Salesforce and has never been on a call, so Zycus cannot yet name or verify them. What the evidence does show about power: BT runs this through its 'Digital Garage' external-solutions team, where Shane sources and screens vendors, with Simen Wefald (Procurement Excellence Director) sitting above him. The move is to get Shane to name and introduce the post-Cyril budget owner before any commercial conversation.
sources: OpportunityContactRole (only Influencers: Shane Gallagher, Simen Wefald)
Avoma Zycus <> BTS Intro, 30 June 2026 (Cyril departure); carried from prior canonical record

decision_criteria.narrative: Shane named three evaluation themes on 30 June 2026: production-ready agentic orchestration (an intake/front-door layer), AI sourcing, and savings-lifecycle tracking that captures the delta across stage gates. He set an explicit bar on vendor readiness: 'ask them what customers are using their platforms on in terms of the agentic orchestration stuff. I'll guarantee 50% of it is not ready with the other competitors' (Avoma, 30 June 2026), which tells us named production references and live customer workflows will be decisive. The formal RFI scoring criteria BT used exist but have not been shared with Zycus, so the weighting behind the shortlist is inferred, not confirmed. The move is to ask Shane for the RFI criteria and BT's must-haves before the RFP forms.
sources: Avoma Zycus <> BTS Intro, 30 June 2026 (Shane Gallagher); carried from prior canonical record

decision_process.narrative: BT Sourced is running a structured, RFI-led process: it issued an orchestration RFI and an AI-sourcing RFI, both of which closed roughly late May 2026 with submissions in and evaluation underway, and an RFP is expected though its timing is unknown (Shane Gallagher, Avoma, 30 June 2026). Zycus entered after both RFI windows closed and is not in the current evaluation, so it is petitioning for a future RFP seat. The internal path runs through the Digital Garage (Shane screens external solutions) up to Procurement Excellence (Simen Wefald) and the unmapped budget owner. The approval chain above Shane and the RFP timeline are the open unknowns; pinning both is the near-term move.
sources: Avoma Zycus <> BTS Intro, 30 June 2026 (Shane Gallagher, Anthony Gray); carried from prior canonical record

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics are in view yet, which is normal for a six-day-old Qualified new-business S2C deal: no MSA/Order Form/SOW, legal, InfoSec, or procurement channel has started. As a Zycus SaaS new-business suite (iSource, iContract, iSupplier, Merlin, iRisk, ANA), a close would eventually need an MSA plus Order Form (RVP signature) and a separately signed SOW (AVP Global Delivery), but that is far downstream. The nearest forcing function would be an incumbent renewal date: BT uses Globality for sourcing and Certio for savings, and establishing those contract-expiration dates would create leverage and visibility. The move is to ask Shane when the Globality and Certio contracts renew.

identify_pain.narrative: The confirmed pain is in savings management: Shane described current savings tracking as an information wash where 'no one can sit down and go through all that,' and wants AI to surface insights across an initiative's stage gates (Avoma, 30 June 2026). He also questioned his incumbent savings tool: 'we use Certio for saving, tracking... is it the perfect tool?' (Avoma, 30 June 2026), signalling dissatisfaction. Inferred pain sits in orchestration and sourcing: BT issued RFIs for an orchestration front-door and AI sourcing, implying gaps in intake and agentic sourcing that the incumbent Globality sourcing platform is not fully covering, though no operational metric was quantified. The pain owner is Shane's Digital Garage / Procurement Excellence function under Simen Wefald.
sources: Avoma Zycus <> BTS Intro, 30 June 2026 (Shane Gallagher); carried from prior canonical record

champion.narrative: There is no champion. Shane Gallagher is an evaluator and gatekeeper, not an advocate: his role is to source and screen external solutions in the Digital Garage and decide whether to advance a vendor to a demo ('if you could send over some materials even so that I can review and kind of bring it to a couple of people before demo', Avoma, 30 June 2026). He has not introduced the deal owner to senior stakeholders, shared the RFI scoring criteria, or pushed back on rivals for Zycus, and he is openly skeptical of vendor readiness ('I'll guarantee 50% of it is not ready with the other competitors'). Simen Wefald (Procurement Excellence Director) and the post-Cyril budget owner are unmapped and unengaged. The move is to develop Shane into a coach by getting him to map the committee and the RFP timeline, then earn a real champion higher up.
sources: Avoma Zycus <> BTS Intro, 30 June 2026 (Shane Gallagher); OpportunityContactRole; carried from prior canonical record

competition.narrative: This is a contested, incumbent-plus-peers field and Zycus is currently outside the active evaluation. Anthony Gray flagged the peer shortlist BT is likely to run in its RFP: 'I know you'll probably be inviting people like Zip and GEP... we're winning in that space' (Avoma, 30 June 2026), making GEP the strongest current peer threat and Zip a live peer participant. Globality is the sourcing incumbent BT is challenging via its AI-sourcing RFI, and Certio is the savings incumbent Shane openly questioned ('is it the perfect tool?'). The wedge is production-readiness: Zycus must counter Shane's '50% not ready' skepticism with named enterprise references for agentic orchestration and AI sourcing, and position the integrated iSource/iContract/Merlin suite plus savings-lifecycle tracking (Insight Studio) against point-solution gaps. Because Zycus missed both RFI windows, the first job is to earn an RFP seat before the field narrows.
sources: Avoma Zycus <> BTS Intro, 30 June 2026 (Shane Gallagher, Anthony Gray); carried from prior canonical record

deal_movement.summary: Brand-new deal: created and qualified 30 June 2026, moved Initial Interest to Qualified and set to New Business the same day, reassigned to Claire Hudson. The single buyer touch is the 30 June intro call with Shane Gallagher; a rep-side 'Next Steps' follow-up email went out 1 July 2026 and is awaiting his reply. Pulse: live but unconverted, single-threaded, awaiting buyer response.

items.change: Opportunity created and qualified same day; StageName Initial Interest -> Qualified; Type set to New Business; Division set to New Markets
Ownership reassigned to Claire Hudson (RSD EU Sales)
Zycus <> BTS intro call held with Shane Gallagher, Anthony Gray and Claire Hudson
Rep-side 'Next Steps' follow-up email sent to Shane Gallagher (awaiting buyer reply)

competitive_position.summary: Zycus entered after BT Sourced's orchestration and AI-sourcing RFIs closed (~late May 2026) and is outside the active evaluation. GEP is the strongest current peer threat and Zip a live peer participant, both expected in BT's forthcoming RFP (Anthony Gray, 30 June 2026). Globality is the sourcing incumbent BT is actively challenging via its AI-sourcing RFI; Certio is the savings incumbent Shane openly questioned. The near-term battle is not price but readiness and access: Zycus must earn an RFP seat and beat Shane's '50% not ready' skepticism with named production references before the field narrows.

ai_fit_signal.summary: No AIS field value is provided, but the buyer signal is strongly AI-forward: BT issued a dedicated AI-sourcing RFI and Shane wants AI to surface savings insights across stage gates ('with the brute force of AI, we can kind of find insights in that', 30 June 2026). Zycus's Merlin agentic layer maps directly, but proof of production-readiness is the gate.

critical_signals.text: Zycus missed both of BT Sourced's orchestration and AI-sourcing RFI windows (closed over a month before the 30 June intro), so it is outside the active evaluation and chasing a future RFP seat against GEP, Zip and incumbent Globality.
The 30 June intro left Shane Gallagher wanting materials to review internally before any demo, with no demo or next meeting booked and a 1 July follow-up still awaiting his reply.

Simen Wefald, the Procurement Excellence Director above Shane, is named on the deal but has never been contacted, and the post-Cyril budget owner is still unnamed, leaving Zycus single-threaded on a gatekeeper.
No pricing, ROI, or savings figure has been discussed; the $150k on a year-out placeholder close is unvalidated and no budget owner is engaged to justify it.

customer_preference.evidence: Shane asked only to 'send over some materials... to review and kind of bring it to a couple of people before demo' with no Zycus-specific lean, and Zycus is outside BT's active RFI evaluation (Avoma, 30 June 2026).

business_case.evidence: Qualitative savings-tracking pain articulated ('capturing the delta throughout the different stage gates'), but no savings target, cost, or ROI figure quantified (Avoma, 30 June 2026).

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Brand-new, six-day-old Qualified S2C deal: Zycus missed both of BT Sourced's orchestration and AI-sourcing RFI windows and is chasing a seat in a future RFP it does not yet hold, with only gatekeeper Shane Gallagher engaged, no economic buyer mapped, and no demo booked.

win_position.text: Zycus is outside BT's active evaluation: Shane Gallagher confirmed on the 30 June intro that both the orchestration and AI-sourcing RFIs had already closed (submissions in, over a month old), so Zycus is petitioning for a future RFP seat it does not yet hold.
No economic buyer is engaged: former CPO Cyril left around January 2026 and the new budget owner is unnamed and has never been on a call, so nothing above gatekeeper level supports Zycus.
Shane is openly skeptical of vendor readiness ('I'll guarantee 50% of it is not ready with the other competitors'), a bar Zycus has not yet cleared with named production references.
The scope fits: Shane's stated need for AI-driven savings-lifecycle tracking with stage-gate delta capture maps directly onto Zycus savings management plus Insight Studio (Avoma, 30 June 2026).

deal_momentum.text: The only forward motion is rep-side: a 'Next Steps' follow-up email went to Shane on 1 July 2026 and is still awaiting his reply, with no demo or next meeting booked.
BT named no dates and Shane asked only for materials to review internally before any demo, so momentum depends entirely on his internal readout.

customer_commitment.text: Commitment is minimal: Shane requested collateral 'to bring to a couple of people before demo' but committed to no demo, RFP inclusion, or timeline (Avoma, 30 June 2026).
Only one of two mapped contacts is engaged; Simen Wefald (Procurement Excellence Director) has never been contacted, so Zycus is single-threaded.

deal_risk.text: Zycus missed both RFI windows and could be shut out of the RFP if BT shortlists off the RFI submissions from GEP, Zip and incumbent Globality.
Incumbent inertia is real: BT keeps Globality for sourcing and Certio for savings, and a year-out placeholder close (30 June 2027) offers no forcing event.

champion.evidence: Shane is a gatekeeper, not an advocate; no champion and no power access.
